author: y4rr
content: Decision: per the dispatch's API-bridge note, ferrotorch's score_mod takes the full [n_q, n_k] matrix (vs. PyTorch's element-wise (b,h,q_idx,kv_idx)→score callable). I built a manual reference inside the fixture generator using plain torch ops (matmul + softmax + add bias + where mask=-inf) so we don't depend on torch.compile/Triton CPU compilation. Cross-checked the manual reference against torch.nn.attention.flex_attention.flex_attention's actual numerics for the baseline variant during fixture-gen. Bias and mask_2d fields are emitted in the fixture so the Rust runner reconstructs the same [n_q, n_k] modification tensor and applies it via a closure that captures bias/mask by move.
